bibliographic_data.title: ONE-NAS: An Online NeuroEvolution based Neural Architecture Search for Time Series Forecasting
bibliographic_data.abstract: Time series forecasting (TSF) is one of the most important tasks in data science, as accurate time series predictions can drive and advance a wide variety of domains including finance, transportation, health care, and power systems. However, real-world utilization of machine learning models for TSF suffers due to pretrained models being able to learn and adapt to unpredictable patterns as previously unseen data arrives over longer time scales. To address this, models must be periodically retained or redesigned, which takes significant human and computational resources. This work presents the Online NeuroEvolution based Neural Architecture Search (ONE-NAS) algorithm, which to the authors’ knowledge is the first neural architecture search algorithm capable of automatically designing and training new recurrent neural networks (RNNs) in an online setting. Without any pretraining, ONE-NAS utilizes populations of RNNs which are continuously updated with new network structures and weights in response to new multivariate input data. ONE-NAS is tested on real-world large-scale multivariate wind turbine data as well a univariate Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) dataset, and is shown to outperform traditional statistical time series forecasting, including naive, moving average, and exponential smoothing methods, as well as state of the art online ARIMA strategies.

core_content.novelty_claim: ONE-NAS is the first algorithm capable of automatically designing and training new recurrent neural networks (RNNs) in an online setting without any pretraining.
core_content.key_methodology: ONE-NAS utilizes populations of RNNs which are continuously updated with new network structures and weights in response to new multivariate input data. It evolves RNN architectures for time series data prediction, allowing the RNN architectures to be continually updated in response to new input data.
outcomes: Outperforms traditional statistical time series forecasting methods including naive, moving average, and exponential smoothing methods
core_content.full_text_markdown: ONE-NAS is a novel approach for online time series forecasting that uses NeuroEvolution to automatically design and train recurrent neural networks (RNNs) in an online setting. This method addresses the challenge of models becoming less effective over long periods due to changing data distributions, by continuously updating RNN architectures with new network structures and weights as new multivariate input data arrives. The algorithm is tested on real-world datasets such as wind turbine sensor data and univariate Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) data, demonstrating superior performance compared to traditional statistical methods and state-of-the-art online ARIMA strategies.
